title: Smoky Smoked Sweet Potato Crumble
description: A tangy, gooey, romantic Korean dish featuring yellow food colouring, roast, and keto-friendly.
instructions: Rub the sweet potatoes with coconut oil, sprinkle evenly with dark brown sugar, ground black pepper, smoked paprika, and cayenne pepper. Toss gently.
Place the sweet potatoes on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and roast for about 45-60 minutes or until tender, flipping halfway through cooking time.

In a small saucepan, combine heavy cream, Dijon mustard, yellow food coloring, and a pinch of ground black pepper. Stir until smooth; it should be pourable like honey but thicker. Reduce heat to low if necessary to avoid scorching.
Remove the sweet potatoes from the oven and let them cool for 10 minutes. Gently scoop out the insides into a bowl and mash with an electric mixer or fork.
Once cooled, mix in cooled heavy cream mixture until fully incorporated and smooth. Sprinkle almond flour over the top to thicken slightly, stirring gently just enough to coat evenly.
Pour crumble mixture back onto the roasted sweet potatoes and bake for another 15-20 minutes at 375°F (190°C) or until golden brown on top.
Let it rest for a couple of minutes before serving as is, garnished with additional Dijon mustard if desired.
